goal: wave21-ui-p1-org-polish: Ship Wave 21 .org UI P1 polish per FOUNDER_WAVE_DECISIONS.md D4 — complete Wave 19 deferred .org pillar-5 only. Scope: (1) knowledge-object-view — add secondary More in Knowledge footer with up to 4 sibling entries (same pillar/type first), exclude current article, derive from existing knowledge index/object list patterns on .org (no new backend). (2) Inline Followed confirmation on follow/unfollow affordances — match .io save-to-context pattern from Wave 19 (brief check then persistent Followed state; useCommunityInteractions). (3) Optional: same inline confirmation on community save/bookmark if same component path. English UI; editorial tone; light default + dark toggle. Run pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build + typecheck. Update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + SESSION_HANDOFF.md. Open ONE draft PR to main.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping Wave 21 .org UI P1 polish (Wave 19 deferred pillar-5) — a secondary 'More in Knowledge' footer of up to 4 sibling knowledge entries on the .org knowledge detail page (same-type first, current excluded, derived from the existing published knowledge object list, no new backend) plus an inline 'Followed' confirmation on the community follow affordance matching the .io Wave 19 save-to-context pattern (brief check glyph then persistent Following state via useCommunityInteractions), and optionally the same brief-check confirmation on the shared .org Save button — apps/web-org only, English/editorial, light+dark preserved, docs updated, ONE draft PR to main
acceptanceCriteria: (1) More in Knowledge: apps/web-org/components/knowledge-object-view.tsx renders a SECONDARY article-footer 'More in Knowledge' block listing up to 4 sibling published knowledge entries — same subtype/type first then others, the current article excluded — derived from the existing published knowledge object list on .org (getApiClient().objects.list({ site:'org', objectType:'knowledge', status:'published' }), the same source knowledge-objects.tsx already uses) with NO new backend endpoint. Each item links to /knowledge/<id> with its subtype label + title. The block renders nothing (no error) when there are no siblings or the list fails to load. Editorial, secondary styling (not a hero); English; light default + dark toggle preserved.

(2) Inline Followed confirmation: the .org follow affordance (the InteractionButton used for 'follow' in apps/web-org/components/community-object-view.tsx, driven by useCommunityInteractions().toggle('follow')) shows a brief inline check confirmation on a successful follow (matching the .io Wave 19 apps/web-io/components/save-to-context.tsx pattern: a transient justSaved-style check glyph for ~1.6s) that then settles into a persistent 'Following' state. Unfollow / toggle-off still works and shows no false confirmation. No toast library or new dependency added.

(3) OPTIONAL (only if same component path is low-risk): the same brief-check inline confirmation is applied to the shared .org Save affordance (apps/web-org/components/save-button.tsx, used by both knowledge and community save/bookmark) — done OR explicitly deferred with reason in the handoff.
English-only UI throughout; editorial visual language; light default + dark toggle preserved; no backend/shared/web-io/combined changes (apps/web-org/** + docs/** only).
`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` and the web-org typecheck pass; only apps/web-org/** and docs/** changed.
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 21 .org UI P1 polish shipped; docs/waves/wave21-ui-p1-org-polish.md updated to reflect what actually shipped vs deferred.
Exactly ONE draft PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready).

tasks.name: web-org-wave21
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement Wave 21 .org UI P1 polish (the deferred Wave 19 .org pillar-5) in apps/web-org/** ONLY, plus update the docs listed below, then open ONE draft PR to main. This is a small, cohesive, low-risk frontend-polish task — no backend, no shared, no web-io changes. Run `pnpm install` first (node_modules is usually missing on cloud start).

DELIVERABLE 1 — 'MORE IN KNOWLEDGE' FOOTER (apps/web-org/components/knowledge-object-view.tsx). After the article body / comments, before or alongside the existing '← All knowledge' back link, add a SECONDARY footer section headed 'More in Knowledge' that lists up to 4 OTHER published knowledge entries. Source the siblings from the SAME data the index already uses: `getApiClient().objects.list({ site: 'org', objectType: 'knowledge', status: 'published' })` and filter to `object.objectType === 'knowledge'` exactly like apps/web-org/components/knowledge-objects.tsx does (KnowledgeObjectRecord). Then: EXCLUDE the current article (`record.id !== object.id`); ORDER same-subtype-first (records whose `record.type === object.type` come before the rest — 'same pillar/type first'); cap the result at 4. Render each as a link to `/knowledge/${encodeURIComponent(record.id)}` showing the subtype label (use subtypeLabel from @/lib/object-display) and the title (objectTitle). Fetch the siblings inside the component (a new useEffect, or fold into the existing load) AFTER the main object is ready; if the list fetch fails or yields 0 siblings, render NOTHING for this block (never an error). Keep it visually secondary / editorial (small uppercase section label like the existing 'Contributed knowledge' heading in knowledge-objects.tsx, muted list styling, var(--border)/var(--muted)/var(--secondary) tokens) — NOT a hero. No new backend endpoint, no new dependency.

DELIVERABLE 2 — INLINE 'FOLLOWED' CONFIRMATION on the .org follow affordance. Match the .io Wave 19 pattern in apps/web-io/components/save-to-context.tsx (READ it for reference; do NOT modify web-io): it keeps a transient `justSaved` boolean set true on a successful (non-toggle-off) action, shows a check glyph for CONFIRM_MS (~1600ms) via a setTimeout cleaned up on unmount, then settles to the persistent saved state. Apply the SAME brief-check-then-persistent idea to the FOLLOW button in apps/web-org/components/community-object-view.tsx — the `InteractionButton` rendered when `verbs.includes('follow')`, whose onClick calls `interactions.toggle('follow')` (useCommunityInteractions). On a successful transition from not-following → following, briefly show an inline check + 'Followed' confirmation, then settle into the existing persistent 'Following' active state. Toggling OFF (unfollow) must NOT show the confirmation. Because the toggle is async via the hook (no return value indicating direction), implement the confirmation by tracking the active/done transition in the button (e.g. detect when `active` flips false→true and the action was user-initiated) or by wrapping the click to know the prior state — pick the cleanest approach that does NOT change use-community-interactions.ts behavior (you MAY add a small amount to the hook ONLY if needed and it stays additive/backward-compatible; prefer keeping the logic in the button/component). Keep `aria-pressed`, disabled-while-pending, and the existing 'Following'/'Follow' labels intact. No toast library, no new dependency.

DELIVERABLE 3 — OPTIONAL (do ONLY if low-risk, else DEFER and say so in the handoff): apply the same brief-check inline confirmation to the shared .org Save affordance apps/web-org/components/save-button.tsx (used by BOTH knowledge-object-view and community-object-view for save/bookmark) so a successful save flashes a check before settling on 'Saved'. SaveButton is currently a controlled stateless component (saved/pending props from useBookmarks); adding a transient just-saved check is fine as long as it stays backward-compatible for every caller and toggle-off shows no confirmation. If this materially increases risk, SKIP it and document the skip.

DOCS: update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md (add a 'Wave 21 .org UI P1 polish shipped' bullet + bump Last updated / summary line), docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md (note Wave 21 shipped, what landed vs deferred, build/typecheck result, the open draft PR, and Wave 20+ remains next), and docs/waves/wave21-ui-p1-org-polish.md (mark Definition of Done items done and record which optional item shipped vs deferred and the final task tree actually used). Keep docs English.

BUILD + PR: run `pnpm install`, then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` AND the web-org typecheck (use the repo's typecheck script if present, e.g. `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck` or `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org exec tsc --noEmit`) — BOTH must pass. Commit your work to your branch and push it. Then open exactly ONE DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 21: .org UI P1 polish (More in Knowledge + inline Followed confirmation)'. The PR body must summarize the three deliverables (noting which optional item shipped vs deferred), list the build + typecheck results, and confirm scope (apps/web-org + docs only, no backend). Do NOT mark the PR ready, do NOT merge. Check for .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md and use it if present.

Your handoff MUST list: how the 'More in Knowledge' block selects/orders/caps siblings and where it renders; exactly how the inline Followed confirmation is implemented (and whether you touched use-community-interactions.ts); whether the optional SaveButton confirmation shipped or was deferred (with reason); the docs updated; the build + typecheck results; and the PR URL.
tasks.brief:
READ FIRST (in apps/web-org unless noted): components/knowledge-object-view.tsx (the page you extend — it loads one ObjectRecord via getApiClient().objects.get(id), has a '← All knowledge' footer link, and an ObjectComments section), components/knowledge-objects.tsx (the EXACT sibling-list source pattern: getApiClient().objects.list({ site:'org', objectType:'knowledge', status:'published' }) filtered to KnowledgeObjectRecord, plus objectTitle/subtypeLabel usage and the secondary section/card styling to mirror), components/community-object-view.tsx (the InteractionButton for 'follow' + the ActiveActions block + useCommunityInteractions usage), lib/use-community-interactions.ts (toggle('follow') is async and returns void; done/pending Sets; do not regress follow/join/offer/act), components/save-button.tsx (the shared controlled Save button — optional deliverable 3), and lib/object-display.ts (objectTitle, subtypeLabel, objectTarget). For the confirmation PATTERN reference, READ apps/web-io/components/save-to-context.tsx (justSaved + CONFIRM_MS=1600 + check glyph + timer cleanup) — reference only, do NOT modify web-io. Load .agents/skills/lane-web-org/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before writing any user-visible copy.

KEY FACTS: This is pure .org frontend polish, isolated and low-risk; it needs NO new backend endpoint (siblings come from the existing published-objects list call). The Next.js app consumes packages/shared via .ts source (no shared build step). node_modules is usually missing — run `pnpm install` first. Editorial visual language: secondary/muted, var(--*) tokens, light default + dark toggle preserved, English-only. Member-gating: the SaveButton/follow affordances already render only for signed-in members (isMember) — keep that. The 'More in Knowledge' block should show for everyone who can see the article (it lists only published public-list objects the API already returns). If a clean typecheck script doesn't exist, run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org exec tsc --noEmit`. Capture a screenshot of the 'More in Knowledge' footer and the inline Followed confirmation if you can run the app; otherwise rely on build + typecheck + code inspection and say so in the handoff. Do NOT touch apps/web-io / apps/backend / apps/combined / packages / data / scripts.
acceptance: apps/web-org/components/knowledge-object-view.tsx shows a secondary 'More in Knowledge' footer of up to 4 sibling published knowledge entries (same subtype/type first, current article excluded) derived from getApiClient().objects.list({ site:'org', objectType:'knowledge', status:'published' }) with NO new backend; each links to /knowledge/<id> with subtype label + title; renders nothing on empty/failure.

The .org follow button (InteractionButton 'follow' in community-object-view.tsx via useCommunityInteractions toggle) shows a brief inline check confirmation (~1.6s, matching apps/web-io/components/save-to-context.tsx) on a successful follow, then settles into the persistent 'Following' state; unfollow shows no confirmation; no new dependency/toast lib.
OPTIONAL: shared apps/web-org/components/save-button.tsx gets the same brief-check confirmation (backward-compatible for all callers) OR is explicitly deferred with reason in the handoff.
English-only UI; editorial tone; light default + dark toggle preserved; only apps/web-org/** and docs/** changed; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` + typecheck pass.
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md + docs/waves/wave21-ui-p1-org-polish.md updated; exactly ONE DRAFT PR opened to main (not merged, not ready).
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` if node_modules missing.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` AND the web-org typecheck (`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org exec tsc --noEmit` if no typecheck script) must pass.
- Confirm `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` touches only apps/web-org/** and docs/**.
## Manual / code inspection
- knowledge-object-view.tsx: 'More in Knowledge' renders ≤4 siblings, same-type-first, current excluded, links to /knowledge/<id>, empty/failure renders nothing, uses the existing objects.list source (no new backend), secondary editorial styling.
- community-object-view.tsx follow button: brief check (~1.6s, save-to-context.tsx style) on successful follow then persistent 'Following'; unfollow shows no confirmation; use-community-interactions.ts behavior not regressed.
- save-button.tsx optional confirmation shipped or documented-deferred.
- Exactly one DRAFT PR to main; docs updated.
## Gotchas
- Frontend-only; do NOT edit web-io/backend/combined/packages/data/scripts. No toast library/new dependency. English-only, light+dark preserved, editorial voice. Siblings come from the EXISTING published-objects list call.

(2) The .org follow affordance (InteractionButton 'follow' in community-object-view.tsx via useCommunityInteractions().toggle('follow')) shows a brief inline check confirmation (~1.6s, matching apps/web-io/components/save-to-context.tsx's justSaved/CONFIRM_MS pattern) on a successful follow and then settles into the persistent 'Following' state, with unfollow showing no false confirmation and no toast library/new dependency added.
